gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/200)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/200)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مَا مِنْ عَبْدٍ كَظَمَ غَيْظاً إِلَّا زَادَهُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عِزّاً فِي الدُّنْيَا وَ الْآخِرَةِ وَ قَدْ قَالَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ الْكاظِمِينَ الْغَيْظَ وَ الْعافِينَ عَنِ النَّاسِ وَ اللَّهُ يُحِبُّ الْمُحْسِنِينَ وَ أَثَابَهُ اللَّهُ مَكَانَ غَيْظِهِ ذَلِكَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from one of his companions, from Malik Bin Husayn Al Sakuny who said,

‘Abu Abdullahasws said: ‘There is none form a servant who suppresses anger except that Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic Increases him with honour in the world and the Hereafter; and Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic Said [3:134] and those who restrain (their) anger and pardon the people; and Allah Loves the doers of good (to others), and Allahazwj would Reward him in place of that anger’.
en.sarwar: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from certain individuals of his people from Malik ibn Hasin al-Sakuni who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) has said, ‘If a servant (of Allah) suppresses his anger, Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, increases his respect in this world and in the next world. Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, has said, “. . . who also harness their anger and forgive the people. Allah loves the righteous ones.” [3:134] Allah will give them such rewards; they had suppressed their anger.’”
